family-prayer.title: Prayer for Family
family-prayer.text:
God of heaven and earth,
my Father, here I am again, 
heart heavy, lifting up to You the people I love the most.
The ones whose smiles light up my darkest days,
whose pain I can feel even when they try to hide it.

Your name is holy... so holy.
You’re the God who stitches families together,
who created us and calls us Your own kids.
You’re a Father to the ones who never had one,
a defender for the hurting and the left-behind.
You love them even more than I do.
So I’m laying them down at Your feet right now... 
because I can’t do this alone. 
I can’t carry it all.

Jesus, Lord... watch over my spouse; 
the one You prepared for me.
Guard our hearts when they get tired or angry.
Teach us to forgive, to hold on tight,
to laugh when things feel heavy.
Make our home a safe place where Your peace surrounds us,
where You're close and real.

Lord, my kids... the ones You’ve put in my care;
cover them, please. Wrap them in Your arms of love.
Even when I'm there walking beside them, be their guiding light.
Give them hearts that run after You.
Block the lies and darkness that try to enter their mind.
Keep their bodies safe from accidents and illness.
Guard their souls from anything that tries to pull them away from You, Lord.

My parents... the ones who worried for me when I was young,
who carried loads I didn’t even see until I got older.
Touch them with real strength. 
Give them health that lasts, peace that settles deep, and dignity in every season.
Where we’ve hurt each other or grown distant, bring healing, real healing.
Help me honor them the way You said, even when it’s hard.

My brothers, sisters, and the rest of my family;
I lift every single one up to You.
If anyone’s sick or in need of You, touch them. 
Heal what’s breaking.
If they’re fighting battles I can’t even name, stand and fight for them.
If they’re lost and wandering, chase them down and bring them home.
If they’re lonely in a crowded room, send arms to hold them, 
someone to sit in the silence with them.

Father... the one thing I want most of all for them is You.
If any of them don’t know You yet, don’t let them stay lost.
Run after them. 
Soften the hard places in their hearts that need You.
Send the right person, the right word, the right moment to show them Jesus.
Protect them when they’re driving down dark roads.
Protect them when they lay their heads down at night and worries creep in.
Protect them from the enemy who wants to steal their joy, kill their hope, and destroy what’s good.
Send Your angels to camp around their houses, around their cars, around every step they take.

Pull us together, Lord.
Where we’re divided and distant with each other, melt these walls with Your love.
Where words have stopped and walls went up, give us courage to talk real again.
Where fear has moved in, kick it out and fill the space with trust and love.
Let our family be a picture of how You keep Your promises and never let go.
I can’t control what they choose.
But I can bring every name, every tear, every unspoken fear straight to You...
and I trust You, Father;
I trust in Your will and Your love.

For Yours is the kingdom,
Yours is the power,
and Yours is the glory,
forever and ever.
In Jesus’ name; my Savior, my King.
Amen.

repentance-prayer.title: Prayer of Repentance
repentance-prayer.text:
Father in heaven,  
I come before You with empty hands and a shattered heart.  

I have sinned against You.  
I have turned my back on Your goodness and chosen my own dark way.  
My sin is ever before me — it tears at my soul and leaves me in agony.  
I groan and weep under the unbearable weight I created for myself.  

Have mercy on me, O God, according to Your unfailing love.  
Blot out my transgressions.  
Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity  
and cleanse me from my sin.  

I am sorry, Father — I am so deeply sorry.  
I am sorry for the doubt that poisoned my mind and made me question Your love.  
I am sorry for the sin I kept choosing even when it destroyed me.  
I am sorry for the anger that raged inside me and wounded everyone around me.  
I am sorry for the running — running from Your presence, running from Your truth, running until I was completely lost and empty.  
I am sorry for the pain I caused — the pain in Your heart, the pain I brought into the lives of those I love, and the crushing pain I brought upon my own soul.  

Jesus, my Lord and Savior,  
remember me in this brokenness.  
Forgive me.  
Wash me clean in Your blood.  
Make me whole again.  

Create in me a clean heart, O God,  
and renew a right spirit within me.  
Do not cast me away from Your presence.  
Restore to me the joy of Your salvation  
and uphold me with Your Spirit.  

I am Yours, Father.  
I turn back to You now with tears and trembling.  
I have nowhere else to go.  
Receive me, hold me, heal me, and make me new.  

For Yours is the kingdom,  
Yours is the power,  
and Yours is the glory,  
forever and ever.  

In Jesus’ name I pray,  
Amen.
